summary: Optimizing application performance and architecture by splitting React contexts to prevent unnecessary re-renders and decomposing the massive Toolbar component into manageable sub-components.

plan.title: Context Splitting Implementation
plan.description: Split DocumentContext and UIContext into separate State and Dispatch contexts in editor-context.tsx. This ensures that components only needing dispatch (like ObjectRenderer) don't re-render on every state change.

plan.title: Component Performance Optimization
plan.description: Memoize ObjectRenderer and DrawingLayer using React.memo. Update them to consume granular dispatch hooks and pass only necessary state props to minimize re-render cycles during high-frequency UI updates.

plan.title: Toolbar Decomposition
plan.description: Refactor Toolbar.tsx by extracting ObjectPropertyEditor, ZoomControls, and ProjectActions into separate sub-components to reduce file size and improve re-render boundaries.

plan.title: Codebase Cleanup and Constants Alignment
plan.description: Replace hardcoded width in OverlayDocument.tsx with CANVAS_BASE_WIDTH, remove deprecated useEditor hook, and move magic strings/numbers from hooks and components to core/constants.ts.

plan.title: Asset and Logic Polish
plan.description: Implement proper SVG path for the heart icon in icon-shapes.ts and ensure all core logic uses centralized constants.

acceptance_criteria.description: DocumentDispatchContext and UIDispatchContext are defined and exported in editor-context.tsx
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Check client/src/lib/editor-context.tsx for these context definitions.

acceptance_criteria.description: ObjectRenderer is wrapped in React.memo and uses useDocumentDispatch/useUIDispatch hooks
OverlayDocument uses CANVAS_BASE_WIDTH instead of hardcoded 600
useEditor hook is removed from the codebase
Toolbar.tsx file size is reduced by at least 30% by moving logic to sub-components
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Compare line count of Toolbar.tsx before and after refactoring.

acceptance_criteria.description: Heart icon in icon-shapes.ts has a unique SVG path, distinct from diamond
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Check the 'heart' entry in the shapes record in client/src/core/icon-shapes.ts.

acceptance_criteria.description: All existing tests or type checks (npm run check) pass
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Execute 'npm run check' in the terminal.

risks: Context splitting might break components that rely on the combined state/dispatch object if not handled with proper backward-compatible hooks.
Memoization might cause stale closures if dependencies are not correctly tracked in hooks or memo comparison functions.
